Neurosci."],"abstract":"<jats:p>The surface spectral reflectance of an object is the key factor for high-fidelity color reproduction and material analysis, and spectral acquisition is the basis of its applications. Based on the theoretical imaging model of a digital camera, the spectral reflectance of any pixels in the image can be obtained through spectral reconstruction technology. This technology can avoid the application limitations of spectral cameras in open scenarios and obtain high spatial resolution multispectral images. However, the current spectral reconstruction algorithms are sensitive to the exposure variant of the test images. That is, when the exposure of the test image is different from that of the training image, the reconstructed spectral curve of the test object will deviate from the real spectral to varying degrees, which will lead to the spectral data of the target object being accurately reconstructed. This article proposes an optimized method for spectral reconstruction based on data augmentation and attention mechanisms using the current deep learning-based spectral reconstruction framework. The proposed method is exposure invariant and will adapt to the open environment in which the light is easily changed and the illumination is non-uniform. Thus, the robustness and reconstruction accuracy of the spectral reconstruction model in practical applications are improved. The experiments show that the proposed method can accurately reconstruct the shape of the spectral reflectance curve of the test object under different test exposure levels. And the spectral reconstruction error of our method at different exposure levels is significantly lower than that of the existing methods, which verifies the proposed method\u2019s effectiveness and superiority.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.3389\/fnins.2022.1031546","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2022,10,18]],"date-time":"2022-10-18T11:42:46Z","timestamp":1666093366000},"update-policy":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.3389\/crossmark-policy","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":9,"title":["Research on the deep learning-based exposure invariant spectral reconstruction method"],"prefix":"10.3389","volume":"16","author":[{"given":"Jinxing","family":"Liang","sequence":"first","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Lei","family":"Xin","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Zhuan","family":"Zuo","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Jing","family":"Zhou","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Anping","family":"Liu","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Hang","family":"Luo","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Xinrong","family":"Hu","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"1965","published-online":{"date-parts":[[2022,10,17]]},"reference":[{"key":"B1","doi-asserted-by":"publisher","DOI":"10.3788\/aos201939.0930003","article-title":"Combined estimation of chlorophyll content in cotton canopy based on hyperspectral parameters and back propagation neural network.","volume":"39","author":"Ablet","year":"2019","journal-title":"Acta Opt.
